title: `man podman-run` shows garbled tables
body:
### Issue Description

`man podman-run`
shows some garbled tables. For example, one table is displayed as

```
 |    host UID     |    container UID    | | -              | -                   | | _fromuid     | _containeruid     | | _fromuid + 1 | _containeruid + 1 | | _fromuid + 2 | _containeruid + 2
       | | _fromuid + 3 | _containeruid + 3 |
```

The same table is not garbled when viewing the HTML version 
https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html

The source code of the table comes from

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/03d7ed468dfcaa883d164382b60306d131e8aa91/docs/source/markdown/options/uidmap.container.md?plain=1#L24-L29

-------------------------------


Interestingly `man podman-run` does not only show garbled tables.

For example this table looks good:


```
       ┌────┬───────────┬─────┬──────────────┐
       │PID │ container │ CPU │ CPU share    │
       ├────┼───────────┼─────┼──────────────┤
       │100 │ C0        │ 0   │ 100% of CPU0 │
       ├────┼───────────┼─────┼──────────────┤
       │101 │ C1        │ 1   │ 100% of CPU1 │
       ├────┼───────────┼─────┼──────────────┤
       │102 │ C1        │ 2   │ 100% of CPU2 │
       └────┴───────────┴─────┴──────────────┘
```

The source code of the table comes from

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/03d7ed468dfcaa883d164382b60306d131e8aa91/docs/source/markdown/options/cpu-shares.md?plain=1#L35-L39


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. On Fedora 38 run `man podman-run`
2. Search for the string _specifies the number of consecutive_, by typing `/specifies the number of consecutive` and press return
3. View the garbled table

 


### Describe the results you received

```
 |    host UID     |    container UID    | | -              | -                   | | _fromuid     | _containeruid     | | _fromuid + 1 | _containeruid + 1 | | _fromuid + 2 | _containeruid + 2
       | | _fromuid + 3 | _containeruid + 3 |
```


### Describe the results you expected

A nicely formatted table

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

```
$ rpm -qf /usr/share/man/man1/podman-run.1.gz
podman-4.5.1-1.fc38.x86_64
```


The other tables in `man podman-run` that have bad formatting are located after these strings

* First mapping step:
* the second mapping step would look like:
* Every additional range is added sequentially afterward:


I see there is also a problem with underscore and italics. Probably that could be fixed with

``` diff
--- a/docs/source/markdown/options/uidmap.container.md
+++ b/docs/source/markdown/options/uidmap.container.md
@@ -23,10 +23,10 @@ If for example _amount_ is **4** the mapping looks like:
 
 |   host UID     |    container UID    |
 | -              | -                   |
-| _from_uid_     | _container_uid_     |
-| _from_uid_ + 1 | _container_uid_ + 1 |
-| _from_uid_ + 2 | _container_uid_ + 2 |
-| _from_uid_ + 3 | _container_uid_ + 3 |
+| *from_uid*     | *container_uid*     |
+| *from_uid* + 1 | *container_uid* + 1 |
+| *from_uid* + 2 | *container_uid* + 2 |
+| *from_uid* + 3 | *container_uid* + 3 |
 
 When **podman <<subcommand>>** is called by an unprivileged user (i.e. running rootless),
 the value _from_uid_ is interpreted as an "intermediate UID". In the rootless
```

but unfortunately that does not have any impact on the garbled table.
